---
title: "Week 11 — Bootstrap confidence intervals"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What values are plausible?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Estimation and uncertainty; what a bootstrap distribution does and
why a confidence interval is a set of plausible values.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Read a StatKey bootstrap-distribution screenshot and interpret the
resulting confidence interval.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new StatKey output;
plausible-values application.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 12 (foundations-bootstrapping), re-cast with StatKey
artifacts.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Curated StatKey screenshot library; no student-side R or programming.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Explain bootstrapping conceptually.
- [ ] Interpret a confidence interval in context.
- [ ] Connect interval width to uncertainty.
- [ ] Avoid common CI misinterpretations.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
